title: Implement planned roadmap lanes #698-#712
goal: Implement the current planned roadmap lanes in order: #698-#703, then #709-#712.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Close the planned roadmap queue by making planning-contract navigation/trust docs and capability-routing follow-ups concrete, validated, and archived.
intent.constraints: Work from the active execplan; keep issue-specific changes bounded; keep runtime/local delegation behavior under human-control config; do not broaden into unrelated backlog.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Implement #698-#703 first, then #709-#712, logging package use and workflow gaps.
execution.proof: Targeted tests for touched surfaces; make check.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make planned workflow/trust and capability-routing work concrete enough that agents can navigate, delegate, validate, and close out without code archaeology.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Implement all planned lanes, and keep logging for reference.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Close the current roadmap queue, not only the first candidate, while preserving an auditable record of package use and gaps.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: One active execplan covering the two planned lanes and ten child issues, implemented in roadmap order with commits at coherent boundaries.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: .agentic-workspace docs/planning state/log/this execplan; planning package CLI and tests; root CLI contracts/tests; model CLI harness and tests; generated reference docs when schemas change.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 25 files across the two planned lanes.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: targeted pytest for changed surfaces; make check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep changes bounded to planned workflow/trust and capability-routing follow-ups; do not start unrelated backlog.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?
